title: figma-pipeline config
description: Single source of truth for the figma-to-code orchestration pipeline. Written by the /init-figma-compose wizard; read by every agent before acting.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to the schema file (enables editor IntelliSense). Not validated beyond being a string.

version.description: Config schema version. Hard-pin for forward compatibility.
Major.minor.patch — e.g. '19.0.0' for React, '3.5.0' for Vue.

config.description: Free-form per-CSS-system options. See .figma-pipeline/protocols/token-strategy.md for keys.

outputDir.description: Where token CSS/JS/JSON files are emitted.

prefix.description: Optional prefix for emitted token names (e.g. '--app-').

fileLayout.description: How token output is structured. 'combined' = one tokens.css. 'split' = primitives/semantic/components. 'framework-native' = the format the CSS system expects (panda.config.ts, theme.ts for styled-components, *.css.ts for vanilla-extract, etc.).

componentBasedLayout.description: Required when designMethodology=component-based. Top-level shared components dir + optional feature/domain dirs that themselves contain components.

featuresDir.description: Optional feature-scoped components root — e.g. src/features

pagesDir.description: Optional page-level components — e.g. src/pages or app/

fileNaming.description: match-component: Button/Button.tsx. match-folder: button/button.tsx. index: Button/index.tsx.

fillModel.description: currentColor for generic UI glyphs; literal for semantic markers. Mixed allows both per-icon.

framework.description: Storybook is the only supported stories framework.

tests.description: Tests split into two independent tracks: unit (vitest/jest/karma) and E2E (always Playwright).

e2e.description: E2E tests are always Playwright. The wizard does not ask for a framework choice — only whether E2E is enabled, and where the specs + config live.

outputDir.description: Where Playwright specs land. 'co-located' = alongside the component (matches unit tests); or a path like 'e2e' / 'tests/e2e'. Set by the wizard's Q-e2e-location. Specs use the component's exact casing + .spec.<ts|tsx> (e.g. ProductCard.spec.tsx).

configPath.description: Where playwright.config.ts lives (repo root by default, or a workspace dir in a monorepo). Set by the wizard's Q-playwright-config-location when no playwright.config.* already exists.

designSystem.description: Higher-level component library that overrides framework + cssSystem emission. When name != "none", component-builder emits DS-native components instead of plain HTML primitives + utility classes.

version.description: Major.minor.patch of the DS library — omit when name is 'none' or 'atomic'.

themeName.description: DS-specific theme key (e.g. chakra's 'default'). 'custom' if the consumer is wiring their own theme. Omit when name is 'none' or 'atomic'.

mcpVerifiedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent successful Figma MCP read (set by the /init-figma-compose wizard at Step 2). Presence proves MCP was reachable at wizard time; coordinators may treat it as a soft cache.

mcpToolNamespace.description: Which Figma MCP tool prefix is exposed in the user's environment. Set by the wizard at Step 2 after probing both variants. Downstream agents prefer this prefix; their tool allowlists list both so they tolerate either.

writeScope.description: Derived from the path-bearing keys above by the wizard; read by check-frozen-paths.sh. Manually editable.

allowedDirs.description: Glob patterns. Agents may write only inside these.

tools.description: Which AI tools this scaffold is wired for. Wizard sets at /init-figma-compose.

codexShortcut.description: Audit of the project-root Codex shortcut. Only set when tools.codexCli==true. The wizard writes an executable wrapper at <projectRoot>/codex-run (chmod 0755) that does `exec .codex/wrap.sh "$@"`. User invokes ./codex-run figma-build <url> — no source, no shell-rc edit, no direnv. Wizard never touches shell rc — see .claude/agents/wizard.md § Step 7.7b.

path.description: Project-relative path to the generated executable (always at project root).

executable.description: True when the wrapper has the 0755 permission set.

skillsInstall.description: Audit of the install phase: resolved skill set written to disk by the wizard, plus any skills the resolution requested that were not present in the scaffold catalog. See .figma-pipeline/protocols/skills.md.

installed.description: Skill directory names kept under .figma-pipeline/skills/ after the wizard's prune. Per-tool surfaces (.claude/skills/ symlinks, .cursor/rules/use-skills.mdc, .codex/skills.md) are derived from this set + config.tools.*.

missing.description: Skill names the resolution called for that were not present in the scaffold catalog. Agents tolerate these — they're surfaced for the consumer to install if needed.

resolvedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent install/strip pass.

complexity.description: Complexity-driven routing. figma-fetcher emits a complexity score per manifest; figma-coordinator picks the minimum skill+model set per agent per tier. See .figma-pipeline/protocols/complexity.md.

enabled.description: When false, the coordinator always uses the 'complex' tier regardless of score (safest default behavior — what the pipeline did before complexity routing was introduced).

tierOverrides.description: Force-bump a tier to a higher one (never to a lower one). Example: { "trivial": "moderate" } means even trivial-scored designs run the moderate playbook.

model.description: Per-tier model overrides. Keys: trivial, moderate, complex, extreme. Values: any Claude model ID. Omitted tiers fall through to the coordinator's default mapping.

thresholds.description: Score cutoffs between tiers. Defaults: trivial<20, moderate<50, complex<80, extreme>=80.

knowledgeGraph.description: Knowledge-graph + RAG configuration. The KG records every component the pipeline builds; the coordinator queries it before each build to enable reuse, prop-shape alignment, and skip-when-unchanged. See .figma-pipeline/protocols/knowledge-graph.md.

enabled.description: When false, agents skip kg:stage/kg:merge/kg:query calls entirely. Useful for dry-run builds or projects that don't want a KG.

storeDir.description: Where ledger.jsonl, graph.json, embeddings.sqlite, handovers/, and staging/ live. Relative to repo root.

embeddings.description: How component summaries are embedded for RAG retrieval.

provider.description: 'local' uses @xenova/transformers (no API cost). 'none' disables RAG but keeps the ledger.

model.description: Model identifier for the chosen provider.

retention.description: How long handovers and staging deltas stick around.

visualRegression.description: Post-build screenshot diff against the Figma source. Disabled by default — opt in once you have a Storybook static export pipeline.

rtk.description: Optional integration with RTK (https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) — a Rust CLI that compresses shell-command output 60-90% before it reaches the AI's context. RTK is inherently user-level (per-machine, per-user) — there is no project-scoped install mode. The wizard NEVER installs RTK or runs `rtk init` itself; it only detects and (when missing) prints per-tool install/init commands. See .claude/agents/wizard.md § Step 7.6 for the rationale.

installed.description: True when `rtk` is on PATH at the most recent wizard or doctor run.

initialized.description: True when an RTK hook is detected in the user's AI-tool config (e.g., a PreToolUse Bash hook in ~/.claude/settings.json). Probed at wizard time when installed=true.

detectedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last detection.

codex.description: Codex CLI-specific overrides. Read only when tools.codexCli == true. Per-size model mapping for complexity routing — see protocols/complexity.md § Per-tool size → model mapping.

modelMap.description: Maps abstract size (sm/md/lg) to a concrete OpenAI model ID. Defaults are conservative; users can pin to any model their Codex CLI version supports. Codex runs the whole pipeline in one `codex exec` session (no sub-agent spawner), so this resolves to a single model for the run — passed via `codex exec --model <id>` when the flag is available, else the global default in ~/.codex/config.toml.

graphify.description: Optional integration with graphify (https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify, PyPI: graphifyy). External Python CLI that builds a queryable knowledge graph at <projectRoot>/graphify-out/ when the user types /graphify . in their AI assistant. Detect-only: the wizard records whether the CLI is on PATH but never installs it, registers the skill, or builds the graph — those are user-level actions (graphify install --platform <tool>, then /graphify .) documented in README § Prerequisites. Downstream agents read graph.json for reuse hints and degrade gracefully when absent.

installed.description: True when the `graphify` CLI is on PATH at wizard time.

detectedAt.description: ISO-8601 of the last wizard/doctor detection.

gitignorePatch.description: Audit of the wizard's idempotent patch to the project-root .gitignore (Step 7.8).
